text.title: technical-debt-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for technical-debt-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tracking debt item count instead of interest reduction — closing 20 cheap items while leaving the one item that causes 40% of PR delay.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Item count is a vanity metric; the business impact is in interest rate reduction (build time, MTTR, PR cycle time).

failure-mode.description: A 6-month-old register with 30-50% stale entries — items fixed silently, descriptions outdated, owners departed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale registers destroy credibility; teams stop trusting and stop maintaining them. Re-baseline quarterly.

failure-mode.description: Registering automatable bit-rot (dependency upgrades, formatter violations) as debt items.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Machine-fixable items waste PM prioritization bandwidth; automate via Renovate/Dependabot and remove from the register.

failure-mode.description: Debt items deferred 4+ quarters with no decision — zombie entries that destroy register credibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every zombie must become either "done next quarter" or "accepted debt" — a positive, explicit choice.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring debt entirely — no register, no allocation, no visibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without registration, debt is invisible to stakeholders and can never be prioritized against features.

failure-mode.description: All-or-nothing approach: waiting for a "debt sprint" that never gets scheduled because features always win.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Continuous small allocation (15-20% per sprint) is more durable than periodic large allocations that are always displaced by roadmap pressure.

failure-mode.description: No business case for debt items — technical language only, no $ or hours quantification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-technical stakeholders cannot prioritize an item described only in technical terms. Every item needs a business-impact statement (hours/sprint lost, incidents caused, features blocked).

failure-mode.description: Ignoring debt entirely — no register, no allocation, no visibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without registration, debt is invisible to stakeholders and can never be prioritized against features.

failure-mode.description: All-or-nothing approach: waiting for a "debt sprint" that never gets scheduled because features always win.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Continuous small allocation (15-20% per sprint) is more durable than periodic large allocations that are always displaced by roadmap pressure.

failure-mode.description: No business case for debt items — technical language only, no $ or hours quantification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-technical stakeholders cannot prioritize an item described only in technical terms. Every item needs a business-impact statement (hours/sprint lost, incidents caused, features blocked).
